title: Quiz: Multi-Reference VQE
description: Test your understanding of multi-reference VQE methods and when they are needed

questions.question: When is multi-reference VQE necessary instead of single-reference VQE?

options.text: For systems with near-degenerate electronic configurations

questions.explanation: Multi-reference VQE is needed when the ground state wavefunction has significant contributions from multiple electronic configurations, such as during bond breaking, in transition states, or for open-shell systems.

questions.question: What is 'static correlation' in electronic structure theory?

options.text: Correlation energy that doesn't change with geometry
Correlation from near-degeneracy of electronic configurations

questions.explanation: Static correlation arises from near-degeneracy of electronic configurations, requiring multiple determinants for a qualitatively correct description. It cannot be captured by single-reference perturbation theory.

questions.question: In a CAS(4,4) active space, what does the notation mean?

options.text: 4 electrons distributed in 4 orbitals with full CI

questions.explanation: CAS(n,m) notation means n electrons distributed among m orbitals with full configuration interaction (all possible occupations). CAS(4,4) treats 4 electrons in 4 orbitals exactly.

questions.question: What happens to H₂ at large bond distances?

options.text: Both bonding and antibonding configurations become equally important
All electrons move to one atom

questions.explanation: At dissociation, H₂ becomes a multi-reference system where both |σσ̄⟩ (bonding) and |σ*σ̄*⟩ (antibonding) configurations have equal weight, representing two separate hydrogen atoms.

questions.question: State-averaged VQE optimizes individual state energies independently.
questions.explanation: False. State-averaged VQE optimizes a weighted average of energies for multiple states (E_SA = Σ wₖEₖ), producing balanced descriptions and avoiding root flipping.

questions.question: What are natural orbitals?

options.text: Orbitals that occur naturally without optimization
Eigenvectors of the one-particle reduced density matrix

questions.explanation: Natural orbitals are the eigenvectors of the one-particle reduced density matrix (1-RDM). They provide the most compact representation of electron correlation, with eigenvalues giving occupation numbers.

questions.question: Which orbital classification is NOT part of the CAS approach?
questions.explanation: CAS divides orbitals into core (always doubly occupied), active (treated with full CI), and virtual (always empty). 'Entangled orbitals' is not a CAS classification.

questions.question: Which systems typically require multi-reference treatment? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Multi-reference methods are needed for: (a) bond breaking where multiple configurations become important, (b) transition metals with near-degenerate d-orbitals, and (d) transition states with near-degenerate configurations. Noble gas atoms are closed-shell single-reference systems.
